quality.rationale: 基于FLRW对称性假设，通过爱因斯坦场方程导出弗里德曼方程，结合静态条件与正能量条件完成多步逻辑推导

origin.question: Problem 19.35. Prove that for a physically possible perfect fluid no solution of the Einstein equations is homogeneous, everywhere isotropic, and static. (Before Hubble's discovery, Einstein considered this a failing of the theory and introduced the "cosmological constant" term as a remedy.)
origin.answer: No solution exists for fluids with positive energy density and pressure ($\rho > 0, p > 0$).
origin.solution: Solution 19.35. The Friedmann equations (Problem 19.15) are$$\ddot {\mathbf {R}} = - \frac {4}{3} \pi \mathrm {G} (\rho + 3 \mathrm {p}) \mathbf {R}$$$$\ddot {\mathbf {R}} \ddot {\mathbf {R}} + 2 \mathbf {R} + 2 \mathbf {k} = 4 \pi \mathbf {G} (\rho - \mathbf {p}) \mathbf {R}^{2}.$$For a static solution, all time derivatives must vanish, so we get two conditions, $\rho + 3p = 0$ and $\rho = 3k / 8\pi GR^2$ , which have no solution for a fluid with positive energy density and pressure (i.e. any known fluid).
